extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco dominated the 70km opening time trial by 43 seconds over ROGLIČ, taking 20 seconds at first checkpoint alone on windswept coastal flats.
tactical_patterns: Evenepoel's 20-second advantage at first intermediate on flat coastal section suggests elite TT specialists can't match his sustained power on long efforts.
McNulty and Vine going 1-2 for UAE before Almeida's ride reveals team depth in TT discipline and strategic roster building for stage racing.
Geoghegan Hart climbing final 2km significantly faster than early starters despite slower coastal splits shows precise pacing strategy for uphill finishes.
43-second gap to Roglič on stage 1 of 21-stage GT demonstrates modern willingness to concede massive time when form differential is clear.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 26-second margin over Roglič at climb base after flat section indicates unprecedented TT dominance regardless of terrain type
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: posting second-fastest time while recovering from spring knee injury signals rapid UAE integration and resilience under physical compromise
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: 43-second deficit suggests either tactical surrender on stage 1 or genuine form deficit that contradicts pre-race GC favorite status
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: superior climb split despite slower flats indicates selective effort allocation and genuine climbing form retention from 2020 win
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: third place 29 seconds down positions him as dark horse GC threat rather than expected domestique role for UAE leadership
